meta.notes: Compliance data current as of mid-2026. Verify with linked official sources before relying on any figure for a compliance decision.

AL: No state law — federal minimum applies
AK: Ballot Measure 1 (2024) schedule; rises to $15 by Jul 2027, then CPI indexing resumes 2028
AZ: Indexed to CPI; Flagstaff: $18.35 (tip credit eliminated 2026)
CA: $20/hr fast food workers; healthcare worker minimum wage separately CPI-adjusted; some locality rates higher
CO: Indexed to CPI annually; tipped minimum $12.14
CT: Increases tied to ECI (Public Act 19-4) through 2026
IL: Chicago: $17.05, effective Jul 1, 2026 (local ordinance only — state rate has no CPI escalator after reaching $15 in Jan 2025)
MD: Montgomery Co: tiered $15.95/$16.50/$18.00 by employer size, effective Jul 1, 2026; Prince George's Co: $15.30, effective Jan 1, 2026
MI: Legislation superseded the court-ordered schedule; rising to $15.00 on Jan 1, 2027
MN: Single statewide rate — large/small employer split was eliminated Jan 2025; CPI-indexed
MO: Prop A schedule reached $15/hr; CPI indexing for future years was repealed by HB 567 (2025)
NV: Flat $12/hr for all employers since Jul 2024 (health benefit differential eliminated); not CPI-indexed
NJ: Indexed to CPI for large employers; annual adjustment occurs each Jan 1 (not Jul 1)
NM: Albuquerque's local rate ($11.85) is currently superseded by the higher state rate; a new city ordinance sets Albuquerque to $13.00 in 2027
NY: NYC/Nassau/Suffolk/Westchester: $17.00; rest of state: $16.00; CPI-indexed statewide from 2027
VA: Scheduled increase to $15.00 did not take effect — legislature did not reenact it by the required deadline, so the rate reverted to the CPI-indexed formula. Separate 2026 legislation (HB1/SB1) sets a new path to $15 by 2028.

paidLeaveTable.p: 80% (est.; up to state avg weekly wage)
paidLeaveTable.c: Not yet set (VEC fiscal note estimate: ~0.75%)

AK.ag: AK Dept of Labor & Workforce Dev

HI.ag: HI Dept of Labor & Industrial Relations

MI.ag: MI Dept of Labor & Economic Opportunity

NV.ag: NV Office of the Labor Commissioner

ND.ag: ND Dept of Labor & Human Rights

OH.ag: OH Bureau of Workers Comp / Commerce

SC.ag: SC Dept of Labor, Licensing & Regulation

TN.ag: TN Dept of Labor & Workforce Dev

newsFeed.title: Maine Pay Range Disclosure Law (LD 54) Takes Effect Jul 29
newsFeed.desc: Maine's new pay transparency law, signed April 24, 2026, requires employers with 10+ employees to post pay ranges in job listings, disclose ranges to current employees on request, and retain pay-history records for 3 years post-separation. It takes effect July 29, 2026.

newsFeed.title: Nebraska Enacts Mini-WARN Act (LB 921)
newsFeed.desc: Effective July 18, 2026, Nebraska employers with 100+ workers must give 90 days' notice (vs. federal WARN's 60) before a mass layoff or closing. Fines run up to $100/day; there is no private right of action.

newsFeed.title: EEOC Regulatory Agenda Targets EEO-1 Reporting for Rescission
newsFeed.desc: The EEOC's Unified Agenda entry confirms a formal proposal to rescind EEO-1/2/3/4/5 reporting and UGESP; OIRA concluded review of the EEO-1 rescission proposal on June 9, 2026. Current filing obligations remain in force until a final rule is issued.

newsFeed.title: DOL WHD Lists 8 Proposed Rules on 2026 Regulatory Agenda
newsFeed.desc: DOL's Wage and Hour Division agenda includes replacing the 2024 independent-contractor rule with a 2021-style test and restoring 2021-style joint-employer guidance. The independent-contractor comment period closed April 28, 2026; no final rule has been issued yet.

newsFeed.title: Virginia Pay Transparency & Salary History Ban Take Effect
newsFeed.desc: Virginia's SB 215/HB 636 now require salary ranges in all job postings (no employer-size threshold) and bar salary-history inquiries. The state AG enforces with $1,000-$5,000 penalties and a 15-day cure period.

newsFeed.title: Virginia Expands Non-Compete Restrictions (SB 170)
newsFeed.desc: Effective July 1, 2026, Virginia voids non-competes for any employee discharged without cause unless severance/consideration was disclosed at signing. The law carries a private right of action with penalties up to $10,000/violation.

newsFeed.title: Minimum Wage Increases Take Effect: Alaska, Oregon, DC + 20 Localities
newsFeed.desc: Alaska rose to $14.00/hr, Oregon's tiers rose about 50 cents (Portland metro $16.80, standard $15.55, non-urban $14.55), and DC rose to $18.40/hr, alongside roughly 20 other local minimum-wage adjustments nationwide.

newsFeed.title: Maine PFML Maximum Weekly Benefit Rises to $1,249.12
newsFeed.desc: Maine's paid-leave program, which began paying benefits May 1, 2026, received its annual maximum-weekly-benefit update, rising from $1,198 to $1,249.12.

newsFeed.title: BLS JOLTS: Job Openings Steady at 7.6 Million in May
newsFeed.desc: The May 2026 JOLTS report showed job openings holding at 7.6 million with hires flat at 5.2 million and separations at 5.1 million (3.1 million quits, 1.7 million layoffs/discharges) — a stable-but-cooling labor market signal.

newsFeed.title: SCOTUS Ruling Clouds NLRB Independence
newsFeed.desc: In Trump v. Slaughter, the Supreme Court ruled 6-3 that the FTC's for-cause removal protection is unconstitutional, overruling Humphrey's Executor. Counsel widely read this as resolving the parallel fight over NLRB Member Wilcox's removal in the President's favor, raising uncertainty about Board composition and quorum.

AL.note: Above-average charge rate; retaliation and Title VII most common.

AK.note: Below-average charge rate; disability and retaliation most common.

AZ.note: Growing charge volume; retaliation and national origin prominent.

AR.note: Moderate charge rate; race and sex discrimination common.

CA.note: Highest absolute volume; DFEH/CRD parallel state enforcement adds complexity.

CO.note: Below-average rate; CDLE enforces parallel state law.

CT.note: Moderate volume; CHRO state enforcement active.

DE.note: High per-capita rate; small workforce amplifies numbers.

DC.note: Highest per-capita rate in US; DC HRA broadest protected classes; OHR active.

FL.note: Second highest absolute volume; high retaliation and disability charges.

GA.note: Very high per-capita rate; Atlanta metro drives volume; retaliation #1.

HI.note: Moderate rate; HCRC state enforcement alongside EEOC.

IL.note: High volume; IDHR state enforcement active; Chicago metro concentration.

KY.note: Above-average per-capita rate; KCHR state enforcement.

LA.note: High per-capita rate; race discrimination most common basis.

MD.note: High rate; MCHR state enforcement; federal contractor concentration.

MA.note: Moderate rate; MCAD state enforcement among most active in US.

MN.note: Moderate rate; MDHR state enforcement active.

MS.note: High per-capita rate; race discrimination most prominent.

NV.note: Very high per-capita rate; gaming/hospitality sector concentration.

NJ.note: High rate; DCR state enforcement very active.

NM.note: Moderate rate; national origin prominent given demographics.

NY.note: High absolute volume; DHR state enforcement among most active; NYC CCHR adds layer.

NC.note: High per-capita rate; retaliation and race most common.

ND.note: Lowest charge rate in US; small workforce.

OH.note: High volume; OCRC state enforcement active.

OR.note: Below-average rate; BOLI state enforcement active.

PA.note: High absolute volume; PHRC state enforcement.

SC.note: High per-capita rate; retaliation and race most common.

TN.note: High per-capita rate; retaliation and disability common.

TX.note: Highest absolute volume in US; large workforce; retaliation #1.

UT.note: Below-average rate; religion discrimination above national average.

VT.note: Low absolute volume; VHRC state enforcement.

VA.note: High rate; DOLI state enforcement; federal contractor concentration in NoVA.

WA.note: Moderate rate; WSHRC state enforcement active.

WY.note: Very low charge rate; small workforce.
